commit.message:
feat: add opencode go and opencode zen provider support. (#28)

This pull request adds support for two new OpenCode providers—OpenCode
Zen and OpenCode Go—enabling access to a wide variety of language models
through the OpenCode API. The integration includes provider definitions,
model lists, and context window settings, and ensures proper API
compatibility. Additionally, the necessary SDK dependency is added.

**New Provider Integrations:**

* Added the `opencodeZen` and `opencodeGo` providers to the codebase,
making them available in the built-in provider list and export surface
of `src/core/llm/providers/index.ts`.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R17-R18)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R41-R42)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R67-R68)
* Implemented `src/core/llm/providers/opencode-zen.ts`, defining the
OpenCode Zen provider with support for a large set of models (GPT,
Claude, Gemini, etc.), dynamic model fetching, and detailed context
window settings.
* Implemented `src/core/llm/providers/opencode-go.ts`, defining the
OpenCode Go provider with a curated list of fallback models (GLM, Kimi,
MiMo, MiniMax) and context window settings.

**Dependency Updates:**

* Added the `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` dependency to `package.json` to
enable compatibility with OpenAI-style APIs required by the new
providers.

---------

Initial release: Rubber Ducky debugging skill and agent

Rubber duck debugging plugin for AI code agents (Copilot CLI, Claude Code).
Forces agents to explain code step-by-step before pushing fixes, catching
false assumptions and logical gaps before they reach production.

Includes:
- rubber-duck-debugging skill (5-phase explain-to-the-duck process)
- rubber-ducky agent (the duck itself — asks probing questions)
- One-line installers for macOS/Linux (install.sh) and Windows (install.ps1)
- Claude Code plugin marketplace metadata

Based on the classic method from The Pragmatic Programmer (1999) and
rubberduckdebugging.com.

escapes double-quotes inside JSON string values that LLMs forget to
escape (e.g. replace(",", ".") where some quotes are escaped but
others are not).

Key insight: tracks key vs value position in JSON structure — keys are
trusted (simple identifiers), while value strings use structural
lookahead (requires ,"key": or } or ] after the closing quote) to
distinguish real string terminators from unescaped quotes in code.

Added 13 new tests for safe_parse_json covering: valid JSON, markdown
fences, invalid escapes, partial/full unescaped quotes, complex code
patterns, trailing commas, single-quoted keys, nested objects, and
the exact real-world failure from QA testing.

All 244 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: support multi-file KUBECONFIG with three-way merge (#970)

* fix: support multi-file KUBECONFIG with three-way merge (#969)

The three-way merge code path used a shallow copy of EnvSettings which
caused ConfigFlags.KubeConfig to point to the wrong struct. When
KUBECONFIG contained multiple colon-separated paths, the entire string
was passed as ExplicitPath to the k8s client loader, causing os.Stat()
to fail with 'no such file or directory'.

Fix by creating a fresh EnvSettings and clearing KubeConfig when it
contains multiple paths, allowing the default loading rules to handle
multi-file KUBECONFIG via the Precedence field.

commit.message:
fix: use SBE-generated PacketHeader (16 bytes) and FramingHeader for UMDF packet framing

Replace the incorrect hand-written UmdfPacketHeader (14 bytes with wrong field
layout) with the SBE-generated PacketHeader (16 bytes) and FramingHeader (4 bytes
per message) composites.

UMDF wire format:
  [PacketHeader 16B: channelNumber + reserved + sequenceVersion + sequenceNumber + sendingTime]
  [FramingHeader 4B: messageLength + encodingType][SBE message]
  [FramingHeader 4B: messageLength + encodingType][SBE message]
  ...

This fixes all message parsing — the app now successfully processes 52M packets,
parsing 18,380 instrument definitions, 15.7M orders, 1.1M trades across 3,639 books.

Fix case 2: under-escaped newlines inside Python string literals

Add _escape_broken_strings() state machine that converts real newlines
inside single/double-quoted Python strings back to \n, stripping
spurious indentation whitespace. Triple-quoted strings left untouched.

Combined both fixes into normalize_edit_text() pipeline:
1. _unescape_collapsed_code: literal \\n → real newlines outside strings
2. _escape_broken_strings: real newlines → \\n inside string literals

Updated call sites in _act_edit_file() and _replan_edit() to use
normalize_edit_text(). Added 14 new tests (31 total for normalization).
All 231 tests pass.

commit.message:
Fix 802.1Q VLAN tag handling in UdpExtractor

InstrDef and Snapshot PCAPs use VLAN-tagged frames (EtherType 0x8100)
adding 4 extra bytes before the IP header. The extractor now dynamically
detects VLAN tags (including QinQ/0x88A8) instead of hardcoding offset 14.

Also adds diagnostic logging to FeedHandler for InstrDef parsing progress,
and a VLAN-tagged frame unit test.

fix: unescape collapsed code — LLM double-escaped newlines in edits

LLMs often produce \\n instead of \n in JSON edit descriptions,
causing multiline code to be collapsed to a single line with literal
\n characters. This broke ALL models in QA testing.

New _unescape_collapsed_code() uses a state machine to convert literal
\n → real newlines ONLY outside Python string literals, preserving
\n inside strings (e.g. '\n'.join()) where it belongs.

Applied in _act_edit_file() and _replan_edit() after JSON parsing.
17 new tests cover basic, mixed, triple-quoted, and realistic cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix COM reentrancy contract violations and re-enable the ExtensionPoints test (#126571)

This PR fixes the `Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints` failure by correcting
CoreCLR COM interop contract handling for paths that can legitimately
re-enter managed code during COM allocation hooks, and it includes a
small cleanup of the related COM visibility helpers. Now that the test
infrastructure change has merged, it also re-enables the
`ExtensionPoints` IMallocSpy test.

## Changes

- Keep `OleVariant::ClearLPSTRArray` and `OleVariant::ClearLPWSTRArray`
as `NOTHROW`, but mark the `CoTaskMemFree` cleanup paths with a
permanent `ThrowsViolation` for runtime reentrancy, since
`CoTaskMemFree` can invoke a managed `IMallocSpy` implementation.
- Wrap the `ComPreStubWorker` setup around the managed exception
dispatcher and unwind handler in a
`BEGIN_CONTRACT_VIOLATION(ThrowsViolation)` / `END_CONTRACT_VIOLATION`
block.
- Update the outer static contract on `ComPreStubWorker` to
`STATIC_CONTRACT_NOTHROW` so it matches the intended HRESULT-returning
behavior outside the explicit violation scope.
- Remove the unused `fForIDispatch` parameter from
`CheckParentComVisibility`.
- Delete the redundant `CheckParentComVisibilityNoThrow` helper and use
`HasInvisibleParent()` directly at the remaining visibility check.
- Re-enable `src/tests/Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints/ExtensionPoints.cs`
by removing the temporary `ActiveIssue` skip on
`Validate_Managed_IMallocSpy`.

## Why this addresses the failure

The failing test exercises COM extension points that can callback into
managed code during allocation cleanup and stub initialization. Those
paths were operating under overly strict contracts, which made valid
reentrant behavior show up as contract violations. This change narrows
the violation handling to the specific reentrant regions while keeping
the surrounding HRESULT-returning contracts intact. The
visibility-helper refactors are supporting cleanups with no intended
behavioral change, and re-enabling the test ensures the original failure
scenario is covered again now that the test can run.
